name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America is a national lender offering payday, installment, and title loans in-store and online, with same-day funding up to $1,000.
description_long:
Advance America is one of the largest national payday and consumer lending chains in the United States, operating thousands of brick-and-mortar locations including this Jacksonville, FL branch in the Commonwealth Village neighborhood near I-95 and I-295. The company describes itself as fully accredited and has accumulated over 200,000 Google reviews, with an aggregate rating of 4.9 out of 5 stars across more than 125,000 customer reviews — an unusually high volume for a storefront lender.

At this Jacksonville location, Advance America offers three core loan products. Payday Loans range from $100 to $500 and are structured as two-to-four-week short-term loans designed to bridge gaps between paychecks. Installment Loans go up to $1,000 and allow repayment across multiple scheduled payments rather than a single lump sum. Title Loans — originated in partnership with LoanCenter, not directly by Advance America — range from $2,000 to $25,000 and require borrowers to use a paid-off, registered vehicle as collateral. The store also offers Western Union wire transfer and money services. Applications are accepted both online and in-store, with phone pre-qualification available at (904) 781-7075.

What distinguishes this location from many competitors is the bilingual service — staff speak Spanish, which broadens accessibility for Jacksonville's Spanish-speaking population. The store maintains consistent weekday hours (10am–6pm Mon–Thu, 9am–6pm Fri), Saturday hours (9am–4pm), and is closed Sundays. The breadth of loan types — from a $100 payday loan to a $25,000 title loan — under one roof is relatively uncommon for storefront lenders. The referral program also provides incentives for existing customers.

Honestly assessed, Advance America serves a real need for consumers who lack access to bank credit but face urgent cash shortfalls. However, payday loans are among the most expensive forms of consumer credit, typically carrying APRs in the triple digits, and the title loan product carries the risk of vehicle repossession if the borrower defaults. The $500 cap on payday loans and the requirement for a checking account exclude some of the most financially vulnerable borrowers. Consumers who can qualify for a credit union payday alternative loan (PAL) or CDFI product at sub-36% APR should exhaust those options first.
pros: 4.9/5 star rating across 125,418+ verified customer reviews — exceptional volume for a storefront lender
Multiple loan types at one location: payday ($100–$500), installment ($100–$1,000), and title loans ($2,000–$25,000)
Online and in-store applications available — no requirement to visit a branch to apply
Phone pre-qualification available — borrowers can check amounts before visiting the store
Bilingual staff — Spanish language service offered in-branch
Western Union money transfer services available on-site
Nationally accredited company with a consistent store network for in-person support
cons: Payday loans are short-term, high-cost credit — APRs are not disclosed on the store page but are typically in the triple digits
Title loans are issued via a third party (LoanCenter), not Advance America directly — borrowers are entering a separate lender relationship
Vehicle repossession is a real risk if a title loan borrower defaults — collateral-based product
Checking account required to qualify — excludes unbanked borrowers
Closed Sundays — limits access for borrowers facing weekend cash emergencies
best_for: Borrowers with poor or no credit who need $100–$500 quickly and cannot qualify for a bank loan
Vehicle owners with a paid-off car who need $2,000–$25,000 and accept collateral-based terms
Spanish-speaking consumers in Jacksonville who need in-person bilingual loan assistance
People who prefer in-store service with face-to-face explanation of loan terms
diagnosis: Advance America is best suited for credit-constrained borrowers who need fast cash in small amounts and have exhausted lower-cost alternatives. The main caveat is cost: payday and installment loans from storefront lenders like Advance America carry significantly higher APRs than bank or credit union products, making them appropriate only for genuine short-term emergencies where the borrower has a clear repayment plan.

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
